---
title: "Sandhill St. John's-Wort (Hypericum tenuifolium)"
slug: "hypericum-tenuifolium"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622221993/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Sandhill St. John's-Wort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malpighiales"
tax_family: "Hypericaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malpighiales", "Hypericaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622221993/medium.jpg" alt="Sandhill St. John's-Wort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/ism14">
ism14</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341758603">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malpighiales
-   **Family:** Hypericaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=635332)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small wildflower characterized by its very narrow, thread-like leaves and bright yellow flowers with numerous fine stamens. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as sandhills, scrub, and open pine woodlands. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is often a subtle component of groundcover vegetation in fire-maintained ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/635332-Hypericum-tenuifolium)
-   [Local profile](https://www.floridanativeplants.com/shop/p/sandhillstjohnswort)
